user_prompt:
Analyze this feedback and extract all issues:

Subject: Speech and Language
Source: EMAIL
Date: 2026-03-10

Content:
I wanted to flag some observations about Max's language development. At age 4, he uses primarily 2-word phrases ('want juice', 'go outside') while his peers are forming full sentences of 5-6 words. He has difficulty being understood by adults outside the family — I estimate about 50% of his speech is intelligible to me compared to the expected 90%+ at this age. He frequently substitutes sounds (saying 'tat' for 'cat', 'wun' for 'run'). He follows simple one-step instructions but gets confused with two-step directions like 'put the book away and then sit down'. He compensates well with gestures and pointing and seems cognitively on track in non-verbal activities like puzzles and building.

issues.title: Limited expressive language
issues.description: At age 4, Max uses primarily 2-word phrases (e.g., 'want juice', 'go outside') while peers are forming full sentences of 5–6 words.

issues.title: Reduced speech intelligibility
issues.description: Approximately 50% of Max's speech is intelligible to adults outside the family, compared to the expected 90%+ intelligibility for a 4-year-old.

issues.title: Phonological errors
issues.description: Max frequently substitutes sounds, such as saying 'tat' for 'cat' and 'wun' for 'run'.

issues.title: Difficulty following multi-step instructions
issues.description: Max follows simple one-step instructions but becomes confused with two-step directions, e.g., 'put the book away and then sit down'.
